one_time_description: Treaty Negotiation Loan forgiven by Government of Canada on March 31, 2020
economic_notes: Nation is party to a Remedial Management Action Plan with ISC due to operating fund deficiencies. Subsequent events include wharf reconstruction ($6,382,440), Solid Waste Management Centre ($1,647,715), Forest Tenure purchase ($9,000,585), and a diesel spill in Rivers Inlet in year ending March 31, 2021. GBEs include Kvamua Enterprises LP (100%) and Central Coast Commercial Fisheries LP (25%). Portfolio investments include BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP, Great Bear Carbon Credit LP, Great Bear Carbon Credit Corporation, and Wuikinuxv Economic Development Corporation.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 statements were not signed until October 12, 2022 — a significant delay of over two years. The $9,269,172 treaty negotiation loan was forgiven by Canada on March 31, 2020, creating a large one-time non-recurring surplus. Total reported revenue on the Statement of Operations is $6,959,275 but the Treaty Loan Forgiveness of $9,269,172 is shown separately as Other Income bringing annual surplus to $9,248,321. GBEs (Kvamua Enterprises LP and Central Coast Commercial Fisheries LP) are accounted for on a modified equity basis; earnings of $142,585 included in revenue. Segment disclosure shows total segment revenue of $16,561,219 (including interprogram and treaty loan forgiveness) which differs from the Statement of Operations figure due to consolidation eliminations and classification. osr calculated as total revenue $6,959,275 minus ISC $1,707,858, Province of BC $1,078,489, FNHA $1,419,251, DFO $198,918, CMHC $91,014 = $2,463,745 (approximate; exact government transfer total used was ISC+Province+FNHA+DFO+CMHC = $4,495,530, OSR = $2,463,745). Liability for contaminated sites of $919,721 recognized. Note: financial statements signed over 2 years late.
